_comment: Provenance for every image used by the guided course. Kit-item photos are reusable across days (course-assets/assets/shared/); per-day diagrams are extracted from the official Freenove PDFs (course-assets/assets/day-NN/). Every entry records where it came from so attribution stays honest (CC BY-NC-SA 3.0). See docs/wayfinder/asset-workflow.md.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — ESP32-S3 development board photo
shared.alt: Official manual photo of the ESP32-S3 development board.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — GPIO extension board image
shared.alt: Official manual image of the ESP32-S3 GPIO extension board.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — HC-SR04 ultrasonic sensor image
shared.alt: Official manual image of the HC-SR04 ultrasonic distance sensor.
Official manual image of a jumper wire.
Official manual screenshot of the Arduino IDE interface.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — Ultrasonic Ranging sketch + Serial Monitor page
shared.alt: Official manual page showing the Ultrasonic Ranging sketch and Serial Monitor output.

shared.origin: Freenove Super Starter Kit for ESP32-S3 — ESP32S3_Pinout.png
shared.alt: Official ESP32-S3 pinout diagram labelling every GPIO, power, and USB pin on the board.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — LED component photo (C Tutorial, Chapter 1 LED, page 40)
shared.alt: Official manual photo of a red LED, its longer positive leg and shorter negative leg labelled.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — resistor component photo (C Tutorial, Chapter 1 LED, page 40)
shared.alt: Official manual photo of a resistor with coloured value bands.

shared.origin: Freenove manual — push button component photo (C Tutorial, Chapter 2 Button & LED, page 48)
shared.alt: Official manual photo of a four-pin push button switch with its pin pairs labelled 1 and 2.

4.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF)
4.alt: Official Freenove circuit for Button & LED: an LED on GPIO 2 through a 220-ohm resistor to ground, and a push button on GPIO 13 with 10k-ohm resistors, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

5.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF; the table lamp reuses Project 2.1's circuit)
5.alt: Official Freenove circuit for Button & LED: an LED on GPIO 2 through a 220-ohm resistor to ground, and a push button on GPIO 13 with 10k-ohm resistors, shown as both a schematic and a breadboard photo.

26.kind: per-day circuit diagram (cropped from the official PDF)
26.alt: Official Freenove diagram showing HC-SR04 VCC connected to 5V, Trig to GPIO 13, Echo to GPIO 14, and GND to ground on the ESP32-S3 GPIO extension board.
